title: generate systemd warning - restart policy "always"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

WARN[0000] Container bd58493110ef5f810c2e6e1242c1b3d8dacd162d0e4d5c67b207e4b55784eaf7 has restart policy "always" which can lead to issues on shutdown: consider recreating the container without a restart policy and use systemd's restart mechanism instead

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman kube play https-pod-pvc.yaml
2. podman kube play https-pod.yaml
3. cd ~/.config/systemd/user/
4. podman generate systemd --files --name https
5. systemctl --user --now enable container-https-nginx.service
6. systemctl --user --now enable pod-https.service

**Describe the results you received:**
Warning

**Describe the results you expected:**
No Warning

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b-dirty
Built:        Wed Oct 19 23:09:30 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
      imagePullPolicy: always
      env:
```

```
  name: https-nginx-volume-pvc

  storageClassName: default
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Mi
```

comments.body:
I see two Pod YAMLs mentioned in the bug, but only one is provided. Are they identical?

Aside from that: @vrothberg does auto-update work without the `--new` option to `generate systemd`?
Nothing in the pod YAML about restart policy, so I have to imagine it's inherent to `play kube` creating the container. Need to verify that later.

Also I don't know if this is correct?
Maybe it did break because I used `--name` with `podman generate systemd --files`
```bash
$ systemctl --user status podman-auto-update.service 
× podman-auto-update.service - Podman auto-update service
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman-auto-update.service; disabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Sat 2022-10-22 22:51:07 CEST; 3s ago
TriggeredBy: ● podman-auto-update.timer
       Docs: man:podman-auto-update(1)
    Process: 25980 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman auto-update (code=exited, status=125)
   Main PID: 25980 (code=exited, status=125)
        CPU: 35ms

Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver systemd[1353]: Starting Podman auto-update service...
Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver podman[25980]: Error: 2 errors occurred:
Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver podman[25980]:         * auto-updating container "6abea61f8486729a81f86e9d0a72492d8d1ceecdeb6858e2bb810231db39be7e": no PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT label found
Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver podman[25980]:         * auto-updating container "a60ca82093da9e69dd12d8fb2c9e2fa71fa70d67160fe47cfc58ed50018041b5": no PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT label found
Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver systemd[1353]: podman-auto-update.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver systemd[1353]: podman-auto-update.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Okt 22 22:51:07 backupserver systemd[1353]: Failed to start Podman auto-update service.
```

```bash
$ systemctl --user status podman-restart.service 
● podman-restart.service - Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman-restart.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (exited) since Sat 2022-10-22 22:51:02 CEST; 8min ago
       Docs: man:podman-start(1)
   Main PID: 25963 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
        CPU: 33ms

Okt 22 22:51:02 backupserver systemd[1353]: Starting Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always...
Okt 22 22:51:02 backupserver podman[25963]: time="2022-10-22T22:51:02+02:00" level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
Okt 22 22:51:02 backupserver podman[25963]: time="2022-10-22T22:51:02+02:00" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 25"
Okt 22 22:51:02 backupserver systemd[1353]: Finished Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always.
```

> I see two Pod YAMLs mentioned in the bug, but only one is provided. Are they identical?
> 
> Aside from that: @vrothberg does auto-update work without the `--new` option to `generate systemd`?

No,  `--new` is a requirement.

> WARN[0000] Container bd58493110ef5f810c2e6e1242c1b3d8dacd162d0e4d5c67b207e4b55784eaf7 has restart policy "always" which can lead to issues on shutdown: consider recreating the container without a restart policy and use systemd's restart mechanism instead

`play kube` defaults to using "always" as the restart policy. `podman generate systemd` picks up the restart policies of the containers and uses them in the units.  So I don't think that it's an issue in `generate systemd`.  

For systemd units I think that the "on-failure" restart policy is generally the best option.  In order to resolve this specific issue, you can either change the restart policy in the Kubernetes YAML or run `generate systemd --restart-policy=on-failure`.

Note that you can run Kubernetes YAML directly in systemd via Podman as described in the following article: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/kubernetes-workloads-podman-systemd

I am closing the issue with the provided solution above.

We can continue the conversation on auto updates and, if needed, open a new issue for it.

@vrothberg Thanks for the response

I'll take a look into the auto update after trying your solution.

But the docs confused me a bit.

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.html
`The default policy is on-failure unless the container was created with a custom restart policy.`
And I didn't specify a restart policy.

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-kube-play.1.html
I don't see a mention about the default restart policy.

Thank you for sharing, @MartinX3!

I totally understand the confusion and will update the `kube play` man page to reflect this default setting.

@vrothberg thank you

> > I see two Pod YAMLs mentioned in the bug, but only one is provided. Are they identical?
> > Aside from that: @vrothberg does auto-update work without the `--new` option to `generate systemd`?
> 
> No, `--new` is a requirement.

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.html
`It does not work on containers or pods created via the REST API or via podman kube play.`

So I am not allowed to use it. :(

> So I am not allowed to use it. :(

You can :)  See commit 274d34a25a3e.

Thanks for pointing that out.  Definitely needs another change to the docs.

@vrothberg thanks for the docs update
I just miss the alternative to `--new`.

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.html
There is the `podman-kube@.service systemd template` mentioned, but not how to use it.

After searching in the internet I found
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/RELEASE_NOTES.md
`systemctl --user start podman-play-kube@$(systemd-escape my.yaml).service`

So I think I should use
```bash
$ systemctl --user start podman-play-kube@$(systemd-escape dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml).service
Failed to start podman-play-kube@dyndns\x2dhttps\x2dip\x2dpod.yaml.service: Unit podman-play-kube@dyndns\x2dhttps\x2dip\x2dpod.yaml.service not found.
```

```bash
$ ls dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml
dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml
```

```bash
$ systemd-escape dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml 
dyndns\x2dhttps\x2dip\x2dpod.yaml
```

Using `$ podman kube play dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml` before didn't help, too.

I also tried
```bash
$ systemctl --user start podman-play-kube@$(systemd-escape dyndns_https_ip_pod.yaml).service
Failed to start podman-play-kube@dyndns_https_ip_pod.yaml.service: Unit podman-play-kube@dyndns_https_ip_pod.yaml.service not found.
```

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: dyndns-https-configmap
data:
  DNSTOKEN: e6e3d827-4b24-4e59-b15b-7ddaa845b705
  SUBDOMAINS: exploding-hamster
  URL: exploding-hamster.duckdns.org
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: dyndns
  labels:
    io.containers.autoupdate: registry
spec:
  restartPolicy: on-failure
  containers:
    - name: https
      image: ghcr.io/linuxserver/swag:latest
      imagePullPolicy: always
      env:
        - name: DUCKDNSTOKEN
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: dyndns-https-configmap
              key: DNSTOKEN
        - name: PGID
          value: "1000"
        - name: PUID
          value: "1000"
        - name: SUBDOMAINS
          value: "wildcard"
        - name: TZ
          value: "Europe/Berlin"
        - name: URL
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: dyndns-https-configmap
              key: URL
        - name: VALIDATION
          value: "duckdns"
      ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          hostPort: 80
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 443
          hostPort: 443
          name: https
          protocol: TCP
      resources:
        requests:
          cpu: "10m"
          memory: "128Mi"
        limits:
          cpu: "100m"
          memory: "128Mi"
      securityContext:
        capabilities:
          add:
            - NET_ADMIN
      volumeMounts:
        - name: dyndns-https-volume
          mountPath: /config
    - name: ip
      image: ghcr.io/linuxserver/duckdns:latest
      imagePullPolicy: always
      env:
        - name: TOKEN
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: dyndns-https-configmap
              key: DNSTOKEN
        - name: PGID
          value: "1000"
        - name: PUID
          value: "1000"
        - name: SUBDOMAINS
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: dyndns-https-configmap
              key: SUBDOMAINS
        - name: TZ
          value: "Europe/Berlin"
      resources:
        requests:
          cpu: "10m"
          memory: "128Mi"
        limits:
          cpu: "100m"
          memory: "128Mi"
  volumes:
    - name: dyndns-https-volume
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: dyndns-https-pvc
```

**Edit:**
I see the typo.
It´s `podman-kube@` instead of `podman-play-kube@`.
But also the https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/commands-demo.md still contains `podman play kube`.

And I see that the systemd-escape forces me to put underscores in my filename.
But it's weird for me, since `podman-kube@` contains a `-` character.

So I tried
```bash
$ systemctl --user start podman-kube@$(systemd-escape dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml).service
Job for podman-kube@dyndns\x2dhttps\x2dip\x2dpod.yaml.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl --user status "podman-kube@dyndns\\x2dhttps\\x2dip\\x2dpod.yaml.service"" and "journalctl --user -xeu "podman-kube@dyndns\\x2dhttps\\x2dip\\x2dpod.yaml.service"" for details.
```
resulting in `Error: open dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml: no such file or directory`

But also I see in the logs `Failed to start A template for running K8s workloads via podman-play-kube.`

So somehow the commands are also mixed in logs and docs of podman.

But I still don't know to to let this service find my yaml.

**Edit0:**
After massive googling I found the solution on a chinese page.
I should not simply write the name of the yaml file in the folder of the yaml file to execute the terminal command.
I need to use the path of my filesystem to this file.

So `systemctl --user start podman-kube@$(systemd-escape $(pwd)/dyndns-https-ip-pod.yaml).service` works.
